entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume crop used for hybrid seed production studies.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A hybrid technology used to create male sterility in pigeonpea.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of seed production per unit area.

entities.name: HYBRID SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of generating hybrid seeds using specific lines and pollination.

entities.name: ISOLATED FIELDS
entities.description: Fields separated from other production areas to prevent cross-contamination.

entities.name: ENCLOSED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Controlled environments such as net houses used for seed production.

entities.name: MALE STERILE A LINE
entities.description: A specific line of pigeonpea that lacks functional pollen.

entities.name: MAINTAINER B LINE
entities.description: A specific line of pigeonpea used to maintain the male sterile line.

entities.name: NET HOUSES
entities.description: Enclosed structures used to control pollination and environment.

entities.name: HONEYBEES
entities.description: Apis mellifera L., used as pollinators for hybrid seed production.

entities.name: NATURAL POLLINATORS
entities.description: Wild insects or agents that facilitate pollination in open fields.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously in the same field.

entities.name: SUNFLOWER
entities.description: A crop used in intercropping with pigeonpea to attract pollinators.

entities.name: SUGAR SOLUTION SPRAYING
entities.description: The application of sugar solution to attract pollinators to the crop.

entities.name: SEQUENTIAL PLANTING
entities.description: The practice of planting lines at specific weekly intervals.

entities.name: OPEN NATURAL FIELD CONDITION
entities.description: The natural environment of a field used for crop production.
